query: What did Bahá'u'lláh teach about the oneness of humanity?
response: Bahá'u'lláh teaches that the "unity of mankind" is a fundamental principle, elevated "above all human aspirations" ([*Tablet to Mánikchí Sáhib*](https://siftersearch.com/document/16692)). This concept is not just an ideal but a divine mandate, urging humanity to transcend divisions and recognize our shared essence. Shoghi Effendi, interpreting Bahá'u'lláh's teachings, emphasizes that world unity is the ultimate goal, suggesting that the oneness of humanity is both a spiritual truth and a practical necessity for peace ([*The World Order of Bahá’u’lláh*](https://siftersearch.com/document/16277)). This vision calls for a profound transformation in how we see each other, fostering a global society where unity is the foundation.

scores.diagnosis: The response is strong overall, with excellent tool usage, citation presence, and authority. query: Find passages about love in the Bahá'í writings
response: Bahá'u'lláh beautifully describes love as "the Beloved’s splendour in the world," suggesting that love is a divine radiance that illuminates our lives ([*A Hymn to Love*](https://siftersearch.com/document/11447)). He also portrays love as shedding "the Beloved’s fragrance upon the soul," where this fragrance symbolizes the bounties and beauty of God ([*A Hymn to Love*](https://siftersearch.com/document/11447)). Furthermore, love is seen as "the Companion of the soul," offering comfort and companionship in our spiritual journey ([*A Hymn to Love*](https://siftersearch.com/document/11447)). These metaphors highlight love's profound role in connecting us to the divine.

scores.diagnosis: The response effectively uses the library to find relevant passages and integrates quotes well, but it could improve by varying the sources instead of relying solely on one document. This would enhance the depth and breadth of the coverage on love in the Bahá'í writings.

query: Read me the opening of the Hidden Words
response:
Certainly!

> "He Is the Glory of Glories. This is that which hath descended from the realm of glory, uttered by the tongue of power and might, and revealed unto the Prophets of old. We have taken the inner essence thereof and clothed it in the garment of brevity, as a token of grace unto the righteous, that they may stand faithful unto the Covenant of God, may fulfill in their lives His trust, and in the realm of spirit obtain the gem of divine virtue." ([*The Hidden Words of Bahá’u’lláh*](https://siftersearch.com/document/8230))

query: Show me the beginning of the Kitáb-i-Íqán
response:
Certainly!

> "In the name of our Lord, the Exalted, the Most High. No man shall attain the shores of the ocean of true understanding except he be detached from all that is in heaven and on earth. Sanctify your souls, O ye peoples of the world, that haply ye may attain that station which God hath destined for you and enter thus the tabernacle which, according to the dispensations of Providence, hath been raised in the firmament of the Bayán." ([*The Kitáb-i-Íqán*](https://siftersearch.com/document/8300))

query: What does Bahá'u'lláh say about justice, and where can I read more?
response: Bahá'u'lláh places great emphasis on justice, describing it as "the best beloved of all things" in His sight ([*The Hidden Words*](https://siftersearch.com/document/8230)). He encourages us to "turn not away" from justice if we desire closeness to Him, highlighting that through justice, we can "see with [our] own eyes" and "know of [our] own knowledge" ([*The Hidden Words*](https://siftersearch.com/document/8230)). Justice is portrayed as both a divine and human attribute, a "gift" and "sign" of God's loving-kindness, meant to guide us in ruling over ourselves and others with "pure justice" ([*Paradise of Justice*](https://siftersearch.com/document/11446)). For more on this profound concept, you might explore Bahá'u'lláh's *Paradise of Justice* and *The Hidden Words*, where these themes are richly developed.
